summary: Manifest checksum validation false negative on empty shard list
description: A malformed manifest with an empty shards array still passes validateShards in src/checkpoint/manifest.ts because the checksum of an empty string join matches whatever checksum field happens to be an empty string too, which some producers left unset.

comments.author: Priya Natarajan
comments.body: Confirmed. createHash on an empty join produces a fixed digest, but a manifest with checksum left as an empty string or undefined does not match that digest either, so today it correctly fails on empty shards, good. The actual bug is narrower: a manifest with exactly one shard whose checksum field is empty string can produce a matching digest by coincidence if checksum was also left empty at the top level.

comments.author: Jonah Kim
comments.body: that's a nasty edge case, good catch

comments.author: Priya Natarajan
comments.body: Added an explicit check that shards.length > 0 and every shard checksum is a non-empty string before computing the digest at all. Verified the coincidence case now fails loudly. Resolving.
